entries: The Syndicate amasses vast wealth through its criminal enter prises, including smuggling, extortion, and illicit trade. This wealth is not only used to fund operations but also to establish connections with influential individuals and organizations outside Corsairea. These connections provide the Syndicate with additional resources, protection, and opportunities for expansion.

Under this system, various criminal factions pay tributes and protection fees to the Syndicate in exchange for operating within Corsairea's borders. These tributes ensure the continuity of their criminal enterprises and provide a source of income for the Syndicate. Failure to comply or encroach on the Syndicate's territories can result in severe consequences, including violent reprisals.

entries.name: Public Perception
entries: While the majority of the population may be aware of the Syndicate's existence, they are often too intimidated or fearful to speak out against it openly. The Syndicate maintains a reputation of ruthlessness and swift retribution, discouraging opposition and keeping the public compliant through fear and manipulation.

entries.name: Law under the Syndicate
entries: The Syndicate has established a set of laws that are not officially in the law books of Corsairea. Breaking any of these laws will meet with a swift reprisal. They are known as {@b 10 Commandmants (or Commandments}.

items: {@b Obedience to the Syndicate.} All citizens of Corsairea are required to acknowledge the authority of the Syndicate and abide by their directives. Failure to comply or show proper respect to the Syndicate's representatives may result in severe penalties, including fines, imprisonment, or even death.

{@b Prohibition of Unauthorized Criminal Activity.} While the Syndicate allows certain criminal enterprises to operate under its control, unauthorized criminal activities are strictly prohibited. Engaging in activities such as theft, murder, smuggling, or extortion without the approval or involvement of the Syndicate is considered a direct challenge to their authority and will be met with swift and severe punishment.

{@b Payment of Protection Fees.} Businesses operating within Corsairea, both legitimate and illicit, are required to pay regular protection fees to the Syndicate. Failure to pay these fees or attempting to evade them will result in the withdrawal of Syndicate protection, leaving the business vulnerable to rival factions or criminal elements.

{@b Non-interference with Syndicate Operations.} No individual or organization may interfere with the operations or territories controlled by the Syndicate without express permission. Unauthorized incursions into Syndicate- controlled areas or attempts to encroach on their businesses will be met with harsh consequences, including violent reprisals and banishment from the city.

{@b Silence and Non-cooperation.} Witnesses to criminal activities must adhere to a strict code of silence. Cooperation with law enforcement officials or providing information that could harm the Syndicate or its members is strictly forbidden. Breaking the code of silence is considered treasonous and will be dealt with accordingly.

{@b Loyalty and Betrayal.} Betrayal of the Syndicate is an offense punishable by death. All members, affiliates, and business partners are expected to remain loyal and committed to the cause. Acts of treachery, espionage, or collaboration with rival factions will be met with swift and unforgiving retribution.

{@b Controlled Information Flow.} The dissemination of sensitive information that could harm the Syndicates operations or endanger its members is strictly regulated. Unauthorized disclosure of Syndicate secrets, plans, or identities is considered a breach of trust and will be met with severe consequences.

{@b Obstruction of Justice.} Impeding or obstructing any investigations or Operations conducted by the Syndicate or its authorized representatives is strictly prohibited. This includes interference with law enforcement, destruction of evidence, or hindering the progress of Syndicate-affiliated activities. Offenders will face immediate and severe punishment.

{@b Enforcement of Syndicate Decisions.} All decisions made by the Syndicate, including judgments, punishments, and territorial disputes, are final and non-negotiable. Challenging or questioning the decisions of the Syndicate is considered an act of defiance and will be met with swift and severe consequences.

{@b Collaboration with Syndicate Authorities.} All citizens are obligated to cooperate fully with Syndicate: authorities when called upon. This includes providing information, testifying in court proceedings, or aiding in Syndicate investigations. Failure to comply may result in charges of obstruction of justice or collusion with rival factions.

entries: The Syndicate also establishes a comprehensive criminal code that covers all offenses committed within Corsairea. This code encompasses both crimes against the Syndicate itself and crimes against the city's inhabitants. Punishments are determined based on the severity of the offense and the Syndicate's judgment.

The Syndicate is the supreme authority in Corsairea, exercising control over all aspects of governance and law enforce ment. Their rule is absolute, and their decisions are final, surpassing any other governing bodies within the city.

Syndicate-appointed judges preside over the courts of Cor sairea. These judges are chosen for their loyalty to the Syndicate and their knowledge of the criminal underworld. Trials are conducted swiftly and efficiently, with little room for legal proceedings or appeals. The Syndicate judges have the authority to issue verdicts and punishments without external over sight.

The Syndicate maintains its own law enforcement agency known as the Syndicate Enforcers. These highly trained individuals are responsible for upholding the law, investigating crimes, and apprehending offenders. They answer only to the Syndicate and operate independently from any traditional city: guard or police force.

entries.name: Money
entries: Corsairea's economic system is a complex tapestry interwoven with the city's history, shaped by its illicit undercurrents and the influence of the Syndicate. At its core lies a bustling marketplace where merchants and traders, both legitimate and shady, converge to exchange goods and services. Prices fluctuate under the influence of supply and demand, but outsiders often find themselves subjected to higher costs, a testament to the city's inherent suspicion towards strangers.

The presence of illicit businesses, such as smuggling, gambling dens, and black market operations, injects an undercurrent of illegal commerce into the city's economic fabric. The Syndicate, with its fingers in various illicit pies, exerts a hidden control over these activities, manipulating the flow of wealth and resources behind the scenes.

Despite the city's reputation for criminality, legitimate businesses do thrive, ranging from blacksmiths and apothecaries to taverns and theaters, catering to the needs and desires of both residents and visitors. Overall, Corsairea's economic system is a delicate balance between legitimate trade, underground dealings, and the clandestine influence of the Syndicate, creating an intricate web where wealth, power, and deception intertwine.

This economy is a very poor economy with almost half of the citizens living in poverty. The middle class comprises most of the tradesmen and merchants (both legal and otherwise) in the city, and the Syndicate's prominent members and those in their pockets filling out the most wealthy.

entries.name: Food
entries: Corsairea's clandestine food network thrives in the shadows; fueled by a web of illicit activities. Deep within the city's underbelly, hidden pockets of farmers and foragers cultivate their crops in secrecy, evading the watchful eyes of law enforcement. These rogue agriculturists grow forbidden herbs, exotic fruits, and illicit crops, carefully tending to their hidden gardens tucked away in abandoned warehouses, forgotten basements, and concealed rooftops. Under the cover of darkness, they trade their bountiful harvests with discreet market vendors and secretive chefs, ensuring the city's most exclusive eateries are stocked with rare delicacies.

But Corsairea's illegal food production reaches far beyond its own walls. Smugglers and pirates venture into treacherous ter sitories, pillaging hidden groves and coveted farmlands under the moonlit sky. They smuggle their contraband harvests through covert channels, using secret routes and corrupt officials to slip past the law. These forbidden provisions, including exotic spices, mythical herbs, and endangered game, find their way into the hands of shady culinary masterminds who: concoct tantalizing dishes that ignite the senses and invite danger.

Within Corsairea's dimly lit taverns and hidden speakeasies, patrons indulge in the illicit feast, savoring the forbidden flavors that the city's criminal culinary scene has to offer. Each bite carries a hint of risk and exhilaration, the knowledge that these delicacies were procured through shadowy means. The allure of the forbidden, the thrill of the unknown, and the satisfaction of dining on food that lies beyond the boundaries of legality make Corsairea's illicit food: production a dangerous and tantalizing affair.
There are but two farms that supply the city with fresh legal produce, but these are under the thumb of the Syndicate, and only those with heavy pockets are able to obtain any food from these places.

entries.name: Introduction
entries: The players are hired by Lord Alric, who is desperate to retrieve a rare and powerful sword that was stolen from him during a game of cards in Corsairea.

The thief who stole the swords is said to be a notorious criminal known only as the {@b Master of Blades}, who has a reputation for being one of the most skilled swordsmen in the realm. He also offers that some other swords have been stolen recently as well. Lord Alric offers a generous reward for the safe return of the swords and warns the players that time is of the essence, as he fears that the swords may be used to start a war between rival factions in Corsairea.

There are several of these swords scattered across the city, each one in the possession of a different criminal or gang. The players must navigate the dangerous streets of Corsairea, infiltrate various hideouts and strongholds, and retrieve the sword.

entries.name: Background
entries: Prominent NPCS in this adventure:

items: Lord Alric Shadowbane
Lavinia Blackshade
Magistrate Fuchiki Dhurn
Phinara
Master of Blades

entries.name: Adventure Basics
entries: We feel that this adventure is a good adventure to introduce the "world" of Corsairea and should be the first adventure played by your group.
{@b This adventure is designed for players level 5-7.}

entries.name: Adventure Begins
entries: The party is brought before Lord Alric soon after arriving in Corsairea. Alric will send some of his bodyguards to collect: the party. If the party doesn’t want to go with the bodyguards, they can either be drugged by a tavern owner who has been paid off, or the bodyguards can set up an ambush and subdue them with minimal effort. However you choose, the party will be be brought to Lord Alric's home. When they first arrive, read the following:

The room is larger than most you would believe would exist in Corsairea. It is filled with art and expensive-looking furniture. A man in his forties with short dark hair and hair on his chin that has been perfectly styled into a small triangle comes into the room, walks up to you with no ceremony and looks you over. He clears his throat.

"{@i You don't look like much, but I've heard about your exploits at (insert some information from one of the party previous adventures) }" He paces in front of you. "{@i I'm Lord Alric, merchant and member of the city council. I have a very delicate situation that needs immediate attention. It requires a bit of discretion. Of course, I will pay you handsomely for your service. }"

Alric will explain the situation. He will tell them that while he: was at the Crimson Rose Casino participating in a poker game, that his prized sword was stolen from him by an unknown person. He will continue to explain that the sword is no ordinary sword, it is one of nine swords held by the leaders of the city and are symbolic of their power here. He also reveals that his sword is not the only sword that has been: stolen.
Alric fears that if word of these swords going missing will cause war to break out among the various factions in the city.
Finish the speech by reading the following:
"{@i “We've heard rumors of a man who's come to Corsairea}", Alric explains. "{@i We know nothing about him, other than he calls himself the Master of Blades.}"

The party can ask questions and he will do his best to answer them. Alric knows more than he let's on, and he intentionally leaves out any mention of the Syndicate, and how his position on the Shadow Council is being threatened. He will tell them all he knows about the Master of Blades, however, it not much. If they are suspicious, the party can determine this by making a {@b DC 16 Charisma (Deception)} check, but they vil not be able to determine what he is holding back.
Alric will offer them a large sum to bring him back his sword, and more to return all of the swords.

If they accept, he will tell them to start at Crimson Rose Casino. He tells them that if they want to contact him, his men are all around the city. Just let them know. However, Alric will not be accessible again until the ead of the quest, and no amount of searching (or even returning to his home) will make him appear again.

entries.name: Act 1: The Crimson Rose
entries: As the party explores the city, they can ask some people about the Master of Blades. Most people will not want to talk to them, either because they are outsiders, or because they just don't know anything. However, four rumors about the Master of Blades persists among the people.

items: The Master of Blades possesses an ancient artifact, a mystical blade of immense power, said to grant its wielder unparalleled combat skills. Many believe that the Master's unmatched mastery of the sword is a direct result of this legendary weapon, making them a force to be reckoned with in any duel.

There is a rumor circulating among the criminal underbelly of Corsairea that the Master of Blades is not just a skilled swordsman but also a master of disguise. It is said that he can effortlessly assume different identities, blending seamlessly into any environment or social circle, allowing him to gather information and manipulate situations to his advantage.

The Master of Blades was once a member of a renowned order of knights, but his disillusionment with the corrupt state of the world led him to abandon his noble heritage and embrace a life of shadow and intrigue.

He possesses an uncanny ability to predict the moves of his opponents, as if he can see the future unfolding before it happens. This extraordinary foresight has allowed him to evade capture and strike with lethal precision, leaving his adversaries bewildered and vulnerable.

entries: Which of these rumors are true? They'll have to find out.
When the party approaches the Crimson Rose Casino, read the following:

The unassuming outside of the casino leads you to believe that this was once an illegal enterprise. The outside looks like it could be any other tavern or inn. The building has no windows, making it impossible to see the inside from the street. Two large orcs stand on either side of the door, screening all those who come in.
The orcs will first try and stop the party from entering the «casino. But any mention of Lord Alric's name will allow them to pass inside immediately
Once the party is inside the casino, read the following:

The place is bustling with life. There are hundreds of people in here playing various games of chance. Table games are spread around the entire place. There are some large men, possibly half-giants, wandering the room and watching for possible cheating. A set of stairs leads down to a basement, and you see several high-class people coming up and down. the stairs.
The whole place smells like a tavern might, with the odors of ale and smoke filling the air.
As you look around the room, a beautiful woman in a blood-red dress approaches you.

Her expression is less than welcoming as she says, "{@i Haven't seen you around here before. I'm Lavinia, and this is my place. You will respect the rules here or you're going to find yourself in deep trouble.}"

If the party mentions why they are there, or about Lord Alric, Lavinia will tell them to shut their mouths. She will get angry with them and tell them that she doesn't want that rumor mongering going on in the casino. She will tell them that people come here because they believe it is a secure location, and that if people found out that something as valuable as Alric's sword was stolen, they'd have less trust in her.

She'll instruct them that if they are only here to question her paying customers that they can leave immediately. The party can then do a {@b DC 14 Wisdom (Perception)} check to determine that all of Lavinia's men have their eyes oa them. A {@b DC 17 Wisdom (Perception)} check will also reveal that there are probably twice as many people working security: than they originally thought—covering virtually every area of the place, and leaving no blind spots. A {@b DC 16 Intelligence (Investigation)} check will further reveal that it would be nearly impossible for someone to steal anything inside this casino without being seen by someone.

Lavinia will allow the party to stay in the casino as long as they don't ask questions. She does, however, expect the patty to gamble. The party can go around the casino and pick up some information. But if they are caught asking questions, they will be thrown out. Anytime they ask a question about the Master of Blades or anything related to their quest, they will have to make a {@b DC 15 Charisma (Deception)} check.

They can also investigate the casino. With a {@b DC 12 Intelligence (Investigation)} check the party can discover that the downstairs (basement) is the high-rollers area, and the place where Alric likely played his poker game.
As long as they are careful, the party can gain the following information in the casino:

items: Alric comes in every Friday night for the poker game with other high-rollers.
Alric usually is drunk before the night is over.
Alric doesn't care about losing money, but does enjoy making interesting bets.
The casino's security is top notch, and they pride themselves on catching any and all thieves and cheaters.
People who are caught cheating or stealing are often never seen again.
No one has seen this so-called Master of Blades inside the casino, but they have heard rumors that he is in the city.

entries: If the players decide to gamble, they can play any games that one might normally find in a casino:

items: Poker
Blackjack
Craps
Roulette
Etc

entries: As the GM, it is up to you how you would like to run these games.
In order to get into the high-rollers area, the party will have to show that they have at least 5,000 gold.
If at any point they are caught in their investigation, the party will be thrown out of the casino, and they will not be allowed to re-enter.

As the party exits the casino (either by choice or by force) they will overhear a two people walking into the nearby Shady Market talking about selling a sword. They can follow the pair into an alley where they will be set upon by {@b 2x Orcs}, and {@b 3x Veterans}. If the party fights, the group will flee when the first of them is killed. But the party will have lost who they were following.
When the party exits the alley, they will be surrounded by city guards and placed under arrest. If the party resists, they can be overwhelmed by city guard and subdued.
{@b Note:} If the party didn't follow the pair down the alley, or didn't engage with the ambush, they can still be arrested.

entries.name: Act 2: The Magistrate
entries: The party will be shackled, their weapons removed, and brought into the nearest entrance of the Catacombs. They will be brought into one of the chambers within the Chambers of the Silent Dead, and be surrounded by coffins. They will be guarded by the city guards fora few moments for the party assess their situation before {@b Magistrate Fuchiki Dhurn} arrives. When he does, read the following:

A slender man with graying hair comes past the guards into the room. He stops and smiles at you, though the smile is a cold one, meant more to intimidate than make you feel welcome. He steps toward you and eyes you up and down, then he steps back nearer his guards before he speaks.
"{@i Alric's choice in henchmen is truly going downhill}"
He leans against one of the coffins casually and looks at you, as if trying to decide what to do with you.

"{@i Till get right to the point. I know Alric has lost his blade. T know he has hired you to find it for him. I know he's given you some sob story about how it was stolen. But don't believe that for a second. Alric is too smart to be taken in like that.}"
If the party asks him who he is, he will introduce himself, emphasizing the fact that he is the magistrate of Corsairea.

If the party asks him to clarify what he says about Lord Alric, he will say the man likely gambled the blade away and lost. He will also tell the party that Alric is desperate to get the blade back because it is the symbol of his status, and that without it he can be replaced as one of the Syndicate's leaders.
The party will probably ask about the Syndicate at this point. If they do, the magistrate will tell them essentially that the Syndicate is more or less the unofficial governing body in the city.
The party can ask other questions, and the magistrate will answer them with the knowledge that he possesses. He cannot reveal the identity of the Master of Blades, nor does he know his whereabouts.
Once the conversation has run its course, segue into the following:

"{@i I want the blade}", the magistrate tells you. "{@i I've made it no secret I want to be part of the Shadow Council, and having Alric's blade will ensure my rise.}". He looks to his guards and motions them to come forward. "{@i Release them.}"

As the guards begin to undo your shackles, the magistrate continues, "{@i I know Alric has made some kind of deal with you to pay for the return of his sword. I'm prepared to go higher. Bring me the blade, and I promise you that you won't regret it.}"
{@b Note:} You can have the Magistrate be a ripe bastard and leave the party in the catacombs to find their way out themselves.

Magistrate Dhurn will allow the party to go free whether they agree to his offer or not. Either way, once they go free, the party will be followed, discreetly, by some of his men. The party will have to roll a {@b DC 15 Wisdom (Insight)} to notice that they are being followed. The same check will also reveal that they are not only being watched by the Magistrate's men, but also by Alric's.

entries.name: Act 3: The Chaos
entries: At this point, the party might want to find out some more about Alric and the Magistrate. If they begin to ask questions about them, they will get the same results as when they asked questions about Master of Blades-most people will not want to speak to them, and the information that they get might be very vague. It is clear that people are afraid to say anything ill about either one of them.

entries.name: Lord Alric
items: Lord Alric Shadowbane possesses a hidden network of informants and spies that span the entire city.
Lord Alric has amassed a vast fortune through dubious means, and his wealth knows no bounds.
Some say that a treasure trove lies hidden beneath his opulent mansion, guarded by traps and enchanted wards.

entries.name: Magistrate Dhurn
items: It is said that he holds grudges against those who have crossed him, and his punishments are often severe and unforging.
Magistrate Dhurn is not just a cunning bureaucrat but also a master of manipulation.

entries: Give the party some time to look around.
As the party is searching, they run into a confrontation between two rival factions in the city. As they are walking down the street, they will hear shouting and weapons clashing.

They will see a bunch of people crowded around what looks like a fight between several people. While some people are running away from the fight, fearing the danger it might bring, others are cheering, one side or the other, on.
A {@b DC 12 Wisdom (Insight)} check will allow the party to notice there are people placing bets on the winner.

If the party wishes to intervene, they can. Treat the fighters as. Veterans and have them attack each other as well as the party. Whether the party gets involved in the fight or not, several other larger men will intervene and break it up.

These men are from the Syndicate, and they are here to enforce law among the fighting men. They will tell them they cannot be fighting in the open like this. The party will overhear one of the fighting men mention something about a stolen sword before he is taken away.

The party is unable to question this man, but they can talk to some of the people in the crowd. If they do, someone will tell them they heard that one of the Syndicate factions stole another leaders sword. They tell you that fights like this have been breaking out all over the city.

If the players search around long enough, you can allow them to run into another of these battles. When they do, you should have the party pulled into the battle. Have someone in the crowd notice them as outsiders and then push them into the fighting. Then have the groups fighting blame the party: before both attacking.

Allow the party time to search the city and try to work outta way to find this Master of Blades. As they continue the search, it should grow more and more dangerous for them. More battles spread across the city, and the party is attacked at random by people on the streets.

As they continue the search also have them randomly roll'a {@b DC 14 Wisdom (Insight)} check a few times. Each time they do, they can notice a figure watching them from a distance that seems to melt away before they can reach them. They can. assume it is one of Alric's or Dhurn's men. Remember, they cannot approach Alric, but if they seek out Dhurn, he will deny anything
Once you feel as if the party has reached their inevitable dead end, they'll be stopped in their search by Phinara. Read the following:
You are stopped by the faintest whisper. You turn and see a silver-haired elf leaning against a wall that you would swear wasn't there a second ago. She motions you over.
She barely looks up at you as she absent-mindedly plays with her knife. "{@i I hear you're looking for the Master of Blades. I can probably help you with that.}"

She has information for the party, as long as they are Wing to pay for it. The information she gives is largely dependent on what they are willing to bribe her with. She will tell the party that she knows where the Master of Blades is, and how Lord Alric lost his sword in the first place.
She will tell the pasty the following information based on how: much they bribe. She'll tell them everything up to their price point (feel free to adjust these numbers as you see fit).

items: 10gp - She'll tell the party that Alric played his usual poker game on Friday night, and that an outsider joined the game, which is unusual. The outsider didn’t give their name.
25gp - She will give the names of three other players at this poker game. Lady Eveline Nightshade, Griselda, and Captain Blackwood.
40gp - She will tell the party that Lord Alric’s sword wasn't stolen. That he put it on the table to bet on a hand and that the unnamed outsider won the sword fairly.

65gp - She will tell the party that the Master of Blades has taken possession of at least three of the nine swords, and she. suspects that he may have a fourth as well. She will also tell them that he is looking to test out his new blades and is looking for challengers.
80gp - She will tell the party that the Master of Blades is hiding out near the Hall of Whispers down in the catacombs.

entries: Once she's given the information, Phinara will add the following:
"{@i If you're ever looking for information, I'm the one you want. I know everything that goes on in this city. Just ask around for Phinara and Pll come and find you.}"

Then she will walk off... seemingly disappearing into the crowd before the party can do or say anything. If the party doesn’t get the location of the Master of Blades from Phinara, they can find him in one of two ways at this point.

They can find and ask the other players about the poker game. Any of them will be reluctant at first, but will eventually tell the same story that Alric wagered the sword on a hand of cards. They will also give hints that they saw the outsider slip into the catacombs after the game ended.

The party can also start spreading word around that they challenge the Master of Blades to a duel. If they do this, they will once again notice the shadowy figure watching them from a distance. If they approach this time, they will not find him, but a note that tells the party to go to the Hall of Whispers in the catacombs.

entries.name: Act 4: The Master
entries: The party can find their way down to the Hall of Whispers through the catacombs. Finding an entrance to the catacombs shouldn't be difficult. They can either re-enter from where they were earlier, or they can ask around for an entrance. Finding the Hall of Whispers might prove a little more difficult for them. If they ask around, they can find a guide that will bring them down there, for a fee. Once they reach the destination, the guide will leave them.
If you waat to have a few encounters in the catacombs, you can have some Bandits or something of the like be in there and attempt to ambush the party.
The Master of Blades will actually be in the room just off the Hall of Whispers. He has set up camp in this room. When the party enters, read the following:

The large room has been set up as a temporary camp with the Master of Blades sitting in the center of it. It is clear that he intends this place to only be a short-term lodging and he is planning to move on soon. As you enter the room, the man is carefully polishing 2 sword as he sits next to a blazing fire.
"{@i Swords are an amazing tool. They are the givers and the takes of life. And all it takes is a flick of the wrist.}"

He stands and looks at you. From beneath his hood, you see he wears a mask, covering his face. It is impossible to tell his species, but he has a fairly human build. He takes the sword he was polishing and he moves it with such quickness and precision, that the blade is but a blur to your eye.
"{@i I know you've been sent here to retrieve Alric's blade. But I won this in a fair contest of chance. I will not give it back.}"

If the party insists on getting the blade back, the Master of Blades proposes a contest. He is always up for a contest and a challenge. He will duel one of the party with swords, and the winner will be the first to have five strikes against their opponent. If the party agrees, and treats this contest fairly, the Master of Blades will honor his commitment, and he will not attempt to kill any of the party members. If the party chooses this method, he will use a simple Rapier in the contest.

If however, the party tries to overpower him, cheat in any way, or attack him all at once, he will use all of his power to kill them. The Master of Blades holds 3 of the 9 Blades of Destiny: Flame Tongue (Lord Alric' Blade), Vorpal Sword, and Frostbrand. He will use these three blades in this fight.

entries.name: Conclusion
entries: However the party obtains the blade(s), they have their own! choices to make

They can keep the blades for themselves. In which case, both Lord Alric and Magistrate Dhurn (and others from the Syndicate) will eventually send people after them to reclaim the: blade, and a power struggle will develop among the members of the Syndicate.

They can return the blade to Lord Alric. In which case Magistrate Dhurn will begin to make their lives miserable while they; are within the city, having the party watched, and ready to; arrest them if they set one toe out of line. Alric will hand over the prize that he promised, and will offer the patty a job.

They can hand the blade over to Magistrate Dhurn, in which case Lord Alric will make his attempt at revenge, and have his men attack you, and will also continue to have a power struggle with other members of the Syndicate. Magistrate Dhurn will give the party only a portion of the reward he promised, and promises the rest when he takes power (which he never will).

An added note, there is the possibility of the party being able to get two of the other swords here. If the party decides to keep any of them, there will be someone coming to look for them, which will cause some problems. As long as these swords are missing, there will also be a great deal of unrest inside of Corsairea, which will effect any future adventures in the city.
